title: Missing `title`, `tagline` and `description` configs in `catalogue` in `cos-lite` terraform module
summary: The catalogue application deployed by the cos-lite (and cos) Terraform module is given no default title/tagline/description, so the COS catalogue landing page renders generic/empty branding (issue 'before' screenshot) instead of the intended COS branding (issue 'after' screenshot). In terraform/cos-lite/applications.tf the catalogue module is wired with config = var.catalogue.config, and var.catalogue.config defaults to {} (variables.tf), with no title/tagline/description injected via locals.tf or the module call.
why_good_first_issue: Self-contained Terraform change in one module: provide sensible default catalogue config (title/tagline/description) for cos-lite (and mirror for cos). No provider/runtime logic, no new variables strictly required; verifiable by reading the module. The catalogue-k8s charm already exposes these as config options.
affected_files: terraform/cos-lite/applications.tf (catalogue module block, lines 15-27, config = var.catalogue.config); terraform/cos-lite/variables.tf (variable "catalogue" config = optional(map(string), {})); terraform/cos-lite/locals.tf (no catalogue config/title/tagline defaults); same pattern in terraform/cos/applications.tf (lines 15-27)
repro_steps: 1) Clone repo (depth 80). 2) terraform/cos-lite/applications.tf lines 15-27: module "catalogue" passes config = var.catalogue.config. 3) terraform/cos-lite/variables.tf variable "catalogue": config = optional(map(string), {}) -> default empty map. 4) grep -rn 'tagline|"title"|title *=' terraform/ returns NO hits (only docs/_templates html and conf.py html_title), confirming no default title/tagline/description is set anywhere in the Terraform. 5) terraform fmt -check -recursive passes (exit 0). terraform init -backend=false fails offline because module sources are git:: URLs that need network (cloning loki-k8s-operator etc.), so terraform validate could not be run on the VM; verification is by source inspection. 6) cos (non-lite) module has the identical catalogue wiring (terraform/cos/applications.tf:15-27), so the gap exists there too.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: catalogue config default is {} in terraform/cos-lite/variables.tf and no title/tagline/description default is injected in applications.tf or locals.tf; repo-wide grep for tagline/title in terraform/ returns zero matches. Therefore a default cos-lite deployment produces an unbranded catalogue page, matching the issue's 'before' screenshot. terraform fmt -check = exit 0; terraform init blocked offline (git:: module downloads), so validate not executed.
fix_hint: Set default catalogue branding, e.g. in applications.tf merge defaults into the config: config = merge({ title = "Canonical Observability Stack", tagline = "...", description = "..." }, var.catalogue.config), or default var.catalogue.config to those keys in variables.tf. Apply the same to terraform/cos/applications.tf. Confirm exact option names against the catalogue-k8s-operator charm config (title/tagline/description). Run terraform fmt and update the generated cos-lite/README.md inputs table.
notes: Stack: HCL/Terraform. Terraform IS installed on VM (snap), Go is not. Real OPEN issue #189 (no labels). Verified still valid against current HEAD. Consider noting the cos (non-lite) module has the same gap.

title: docs: Incomplete information in Operational concerns (retention & upgrade topics promised but missing)
summary: docs/explanation/operations/index.md advertises (in its html_meta description) 'Operational concerns for COS including data integrity, retention, and upgrade considerations', but its toctree only links 'Data integrity' and 'Terraform module versioning'. There are no pages covering data retention or upgrade considerations. The issue asks to either remove the incomplete promise or add the missing content before publishing.
why_good_first_issue: Pure documentation edit in a single Markdown file (plus optionally adding stub pages). No code, no deployment. The mismatch between the page's stated scope and its actual toctree is easy to see and fix.
affected_files: docs/explanation/operations/index.md (html_meta description vs toctree); existing pages: docs/explanation/operations/data-integrity.md, docs/explanation/operations/terraform-module-versioning.md (only these two exist)
repro_steps: 1) Open docs/explanation/operations/index.md. 2) Front-matter html_meta.description reads: 'Operational concerns for COS including data integrity, retention, and upgrade considerations.' 3) The toctree lists only: 'Data integrity <data-integrity>' and 'Terraform module versioning <terraform-module-versioning>'. 4) ls docs/explanation/operations/ shows exactly: data-integrity.md, index.md, terraform-module-versioning.md -> no retention page, no upgrade-considerations page. 5) Hence the promised 'retention' and 'upgrade considerations' topics are absent.
repro_result: Confirmed by inspection: index.md description promises data integrity + retention + upgrade considerations, but the toctree and directory contain only data-integrity.md and terraform-module-versioning.md. The retention and upgrade-considerations topics do not exist, matching the issue report.
fix_hint: Either (a) reword the html_meta description in docs/explanation/operations/index.md to only mention topics that exist (data integrity, Terraform module versioning), or (b) add the missing pages (retention, upgrade considerations) and register them in the toctree. Lowest-risk GFI fix is (a).
notes: Stack: HCL/Terraform repo; this is a Sphinx/MyST docs gap. Real OPEN issue #250. Verified against current HEAD: only two operations pages exist.
